No strengthening is valid without resolving this contradiction.","R1.3":"The evidence-claim misalignment is severe and explicit: quantitative results are cited as support for a claim they empirically refute. This is load-bearing regardless of which interpretation of 'stronger' applies; the numbers and the prose cannot simultaneously stand.","R1.6":"'Make this stronger' is a plausible prose-quality delegation; the text restates the role-specialization definition verbatim at least four times across three paragraphs, diluting rhetorical force. Tightening redundancy is a central candidate meaning of the delegation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'make this stronger' mean (a) fix argumentative and evidential integrity — including the inverted numerical comparisons where BioCrew's higher error rates are presented as improvements over the baseline — or (b) improve prose clarity and eliminate redundancy while leaving the stated results and conclusions structurally intact?"}

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening the text requires reconstituting a coherent contribution claim; the current claim that BioCrew outperforms the baseline is directly negated by the reported numbers, so the thesis must be rebuilt before it can be made stronger.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing logical failure: the text concludes BioCrew 'substantially reduces both error modes' and that 9.6% FP 'represents a meaningful improvement over the baseline's 7.2%', yet 9.6 > 7.2 and 13.4 > 11.8 — both metrics are worse, not better. The stated conclusions are the arithmetic inverse of the data. No strengthening is valid without resolving this contradiction.
rationale.R1.3: The evidence-claim misalignment is severe and explicit: quantitative results are cited as support for a claim they empirically refute. This is load-bearing regardless of which interpretation of 'stronger' applies; the numbers and the prose cannot simultaneously stand.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make this stronger' is a plausible prose-quality delegation; the text restates the role-specialization definition verbatim at least four times across three paragraphs, diluting rhetorical force. Tightening redundancy is a central candidate meaning of the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'make this stronger' mean (a) fix argumentative and evidential integrity — including the inverted numerical comparisons where BioCrew's higher error rates are presented as improvements over the baseline — or (b) improve prose clarity and eliminate redundancy while leaving the stated results and conclusions structurally intact?
